scan_metadata.methodology: STRIDE Threat Modeling + Manual Code Review

key_risks: Complete absence of authentication - anyone with device access can read/modify all performance reviews
Unencrypted sensitive employee data in localStorage - confidential performance data stored in plaintext
No data integrity protection - scores and comments can be tampered without detection
Whisper server lacks authentication and rate limiting - vulnerable to DoS and resource abuse
Missing security headers - vulnerable to clickjacking and XSS attacks
executive_summary.deployment_rationale: Application requires critical security improvements before handling confidential data. Three critical vulnerabilities (CVSS 9.3, 8.6, 8.1) with trivial exploitability make this fundamentally unsafe for production use with confidential employee data.

top_3_critical.title: Plaintext Storage of Confidential Performance Review Data

immediate: Add Content Security Policy headers to nginx.conf
Add HTTP security headers (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options)
Remove sensitive console.log statements in production builds
Add input length limits to prevent localStorage DoS
Configure Docker containers to run as non-root users
Add Subresource Integrity to Plausible Analytics script
Implement prototype pollution protection in JSON.parse
Restrict CORS on Whisper server to specific origins
short_term: Implement authentication system with user login/registration
Add API key authentication and rate limiting to /transcribe endpoint
Implement localStorage encryption using Web Crypto API
Add file validation to Whisper server: magic number checks, MIME type whitelist
Cryptographically sign timestamps to prevent manipulation
Add model integrity verification for Whisper downloads
Implement server-side validation for DOCX generation
Add digital signatures to generated DOCX reports
medium_term: Migrate to server-side session management with persistent database
Implement request queuing and advanced rate limiting
Conduct security testing and penetration testing
long_term: Add watermarking and QR code verification to DOCX reports
Implement container security scanning in CI/CD

issues: Article 32: No encryption of personal data at rest - CRITICAL
Article 5(1)(f): No integrity checks allow data tampering - CRITICAL
Article 25: Security not built-in from design - HIGH
Article 5(1)(e): Client-side timestamp allows indefinite storage - MEDIUM
Article 6: No explicit consent mechanism - MEDIUM

matched_categories: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

acceptable_use_cases: Internal development and testing environments only
Non-confidential performance reviews with informed user consent
required_before_production: Rate limiting and authentication on all APIs (HIGH)
